title: Lyran IV

'''Lyran IV''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Agriworld/Legends|farming planet]] located in the [[Quiberon sector/Legends|Quiberon sector]]. It was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Parasite/Legends|parasites]] known as [[Lyran bloodslug]]s. The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] served as a notable trans-shipment point of illegal [[Spice/Legends|spice]], with the local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]] investing into the local [[Farm/Legends|agricultural]] communities in order to maintain cover for their illicit operations.

==Description==
Lyran IV, also referred to simply as Lyran, was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="Wizards" /> in the [[Lyran system/Legends|Lyran system]], a part of the [[Quiberon sector/Legends|Quiberon sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was situated on the [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]] known as the [[Triellus Trade Route/Legends|Triellus Trade Route]],<ref name="TEA" /> which linked it to the [[Molavar system/Legends|Molavar system]] in the [[Abrion sector/Legends|Abrion sector]] as well as the [[Galov sector/Legends|Galov sector's]] [[Opoku system]].<ref name="OuterRim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Lyran system/Legends|Lyran system]]</ref>

Lyran IV was an abundant [[Agriworld/Legends|farming world]] known for its rolling plains where [[Wheat/Legends|wheat]] was [[Farm/Legends|grown]]. The planet was also [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Lyran bloodslug]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of small [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] [[Parasite/Legends|parasite]] that burrowed its way into the digestive tract of larger creatures, such as the vermin found in Lyran IV's [[Grain/Legends|grain]] silos and warehouses.<ref name="Wizards" />

==Inhabitants==
A sleepy backwater world that had a feudal government and exported agricultural produce, Lyran IV<ref name="Wizards" /> also served as a notable trans-shipment point of illegal [[Spice/Legends|spice]]<ref name="TEA" /> such as [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]].<ref name="Wizards" /> Between [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]] and [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Dates">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20011213c_slug&c=rpg|series=Creature Feature|text=Lyran Bloodslug}} establishes that Lyran IV hosted a population of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] during the [[Rise of the Empire era]], which the [[Wookieepedia:The Star Wars Legends Novels Timeline|''Star Wars'' Legends Novels Timeline]] dates to between [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]] and [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].</ref> the planet hosted a small but active population of criminals, leading it to be regarded as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler's]] paradise. A steady supply of illegal substances was regularly brought from the [[Kessel system/Legends|Kessel]] and [[Ryloth system/Legends|Ryloth systems]] and stored at smuggler bases on Lyran IV. The substances were subsequently smuggled aboard [[Starship/Legends|starships]] exporting innocuous cargoes of grain and sold throughout the Outer Rim.<ref name="Wizards" />

To create cover for their illegal operations, the local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]] invested heavily in the local farming communities and made a marginal but steady profit on agricultural sales. The [[Farmer/Legends|peasants]] of Lyran IV believed it was somewhat ironic that the criminals were exploiting them, similarly to how Lyran bloodslugs fed off their hosts. The [[Slug/Legends|slugs]] were also used by the crime lords as a method of [[Death/Legends|assassinating]] undesirable [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]]—the creatures were hidden in the cargo of a departing starship, where they would eventually burrow themselves into the vessel's [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]].<ref name="Wizards" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lyran IV was introduced in "Lyran Bloodslug," an article in the ''[[Creature Feature]]'' series that was written by [[Brian Campbell]] and published on [[Wizards.com]] on [[December 13]], [[2001]].<ref name="Wizards" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Lyran [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Lyran IV, in grid square S-15.<ref name="TEA" />
